content.full_text:
FEB 11 1031 AM '91

KATHLEEN BROWN
Treasurer of the State of California

February 6, 1991

Blanca Breeze
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J St. #800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Blanca,

This is to confirm my understanding of the conversation we had
last week concerning the requirements of mass mailings by elected
officials.  I would appreciate your response with corrections if
I am wrong.

At specific issue here is a program established by the State
Treasurer called the College Savings Bond Program.  This is a
program wherein we are trying to get citizens to buy certain
bonds which can be redeemed in the future hopefully to be used
for college expenses.

To operate the program, we must advertise and subsequently, send
information on how to apply.  Immediately after the
advertisement, the responses would be "solicited."  However, much
later in the year, we often receive "unsolicited" inquiries about
the program.

As it concerns the solicited responses, it is my understanding
that we can send a brochure/pamphlet that has the seal of the
Office of the State Treasurer on it and/or has unsigned letter-
head stationary as part of the design of the brochure/pamphlet.
It would also be allowable to send a brochure/pamphlet which only
has the seal, however, it can be in an envelope along with a
letter on letter-head stationery, signed by a non-elected
official, but not signed by the Treasurer herself.  The envelope
can have the seal of the Office of State Treasurer, but not the
Treasurer's name or the words "State Treasurer."  (However, on
this last point, section (b) (1) of Section 189001 seems to say
that mass mailings of items is not prohibited in envelopes of the
agency on which the elected officer's name appears--please
clarify.)

915 CAPITOL MALL · SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 · (916) 445-5316

[The image shows a mostly blank page with a circular seal/stamp at the top center. The seal appears to contain text around its perimeter and some kind of emblem or image in the center, but the text is too small and blurry to read with certainty.]

Ms. Blanca Breeze
February 6, 1991
Page Two

As it concerns unsolicited requests, these would not constitute a
"mass mailing" and none of the restrictions on featuring an
elected officer apply. Consequently, the name, picture and
signature can be in the information sent back to the requestor.

Sincerely,

[signature]
HAL GEIOGUE
Assistant Treasurer

This is a program wherein we are trying to get citizens to buy certain bonds which can be redeemed in the future hopefully to be used for college expenses. To operate the program, we must advertise and subsequently, send information on how to apply. Immediately after the advertisement, the responses would be "solicited." As it concerns the solicited responses, it is my understanding that we can send a brochure/pamphlet that has the seal of the Office of the State Treasurer on it and/or has unsigned letter-head stationary as part of the design of the brochure/pamphlet. The image you provided is mostly blank, with only a small stamp or seal visible in the upper right corner. The stamp appears to be from the University of Pennsylvania Library, as indicated by the text and design on it. There is no additional text or content on the page aside from this stamp. If you have another page or section of the document that you would like me to analyze, please provide it. Ms. Blanca Breeze February 6, 1991 Page Two As it concerns unsolicited requests, these would not constitute a "mass mailing" and none of the restrictions on featuring an elected officer apply. Consequently, the name, picture and signature can be in the information sent back to the requestor. Sincerely, HAL GEIOGUE Assistant Treasurer
